name: Constant Display
classification_notes: Described as a modern sans serif inspired by the lemniscate, with glyphs designed to extend and connect fluidly; the expressive, movement-driven construction places it firmly in display territory rather than general-purpose sans.
summary: A geometric display sans by Will Hasleham, built around the lemniscate curve to give glyphs a sense of continuous, looping movement.
description: Drawn by Will Hasleham for Type Department, the design takes the lemniscate as its structural reference point, allowing letterforms to appear to extend and connect across a composition. The result is a single-weight display face with a fluid, almost kinetic quality suited to logotypes, headlines and short-form typographic work. Coverage is modest at 101 glyphs, covering uppercase, lowercase, figures and basic punctuation across Latin.
foundry_blurb: Type Department describe Constant Display as a modern and dynamic sans serif inspired by the lemniscate, with glyphs designed to extend and connect to create a subtle sense of movement and fluidity throughout typographic compositions.

name: Tenebras
classification_notes: An uppercase-only display typeface with dynamic curves and historical ornamental influence drawn from Art Nouveau and the 1900 typeface Edda by Heinrich Heinz Heune, placing it firmly in the decorative display category.
summary: An uppercase-only display face by Domenico Barréto, drawing on Art Nouveau and the 1900 German typeface Edda by Heinrich Heinz Heune.
description: The letterforms carry the dynamic curves and ornamental warmth of turn-of-the-century Art Nouveau, filtered through a contemporary sensibility suited to editorial headlines and title work. It is uppercase only with a 303-glyph set covering extended Latin diacritics, numerals, fractions and symbols. Best suited to logotypes, magazine covers and short-form display settings where visual weight and historical character are the point.
foundry_blurb: Type Department describe Tenebras as a striking uppercase display typeface inspired by Art Nouveau and Edda, the 1900 typeface by Heinrich Heinz Heune. They note its dynamic curves and blend of historical charm with contemporary design, positioning it for elegant titles, eye-catching headlines and projects demanding sophistication and visual impact.

name: Grotta
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a neo-Grotesk with geometric accents and tight apertures, placing it firmly in the neo-grotesque tradition rather than a pure geometric or humanist sans.
summary: A neo-grotesque with geometric accents and tight apertures across five weights, updated in 2024 with new italic styles.
description: Grotta is a neo-grotesque designed by Due Studio, characterised by sharp contrasts, strong geometric details and tight apertures that give the family a taut, energetic quality. Five weights run from Light to Bold, each with a matching italic added or revised in a 2024 update, bringing the family to ten styles in total. A glyph set of 599 characters covers Latin Extended-A alongside OpenType features including contextual alternates, stylistic alternates and localised forms.
foundry_blurb: Type Department describe Grotta as a bold and contemporary neo-Grotesk typeface family, characterised by strong geometric accents, sharp contrasts and tight apertures that give it a dynamic and energetic feel. They position it as capturing the essence of shifting contemporary aesthetics and evolving design sensibilities, with applications spanning modern editorial layouts, impactful branding and digital interfaces.

name: Brier
classification_notes: A display serif with humanist letterforms and decorative broken strokes in the crossbars, drawing on botanical motifs. The design prioritises elegance at large sizes rather than text-setting utility.
summary: A botanically-inspired display serif by Fatima Abbas, distinguished by broken crossbar strokes and humanist proportions across two weights.
description: Designed by Fatima Abbas and first released in March 2021, Brier draws on botanical references to inform its broken crossbar strokes and graceful letterforms. The family offers Regular and Bold weights, each with over 380 glyphs including stylistic alternates, diacritics and ligatures. It suits editorial layouts, branding and decorative applications where a serif with an organic sensibility is needed at display sizes.
foundry_blurb: Type Department describe Brier as a sophisticated serif display typeface primarily inspired by elements of nature, with botanical influences evident in its delicate broken crossbar strokes and humanist letterforms. Available in Regular and Bold, each weight includes over 380 glyphs with stylistic alternates, diacritics and ligatures, making it suited to editorial, branding and decorative work.

name: T1 Korium
classification_notes: Korium blends humanist letterform principles with tightly condensed, angular forms, placing it within the humanist sans category despite its assertive, display-oriented character.
summary: A variable humanist sans by Valerio Monopoli with tightly condensed, angular forms and a thematic character set drawn from nuclear symbolism.
description: Developed by TYPE01 in collaboration with Valerio Monopoli, Korium is a variable font spanning six weight steps from 1KG to 6KG. Its condensed silhouette and angular construction give it a forceful presence suited to display and identity work. The character set runs to over 400 glyphs and includes wayfinding arrows, atom, biohazard and radioactivity symbols, chemical signs and further Unicode easter eggs inspired by the nuclear material corium.
foundry_blurb: TYPE01 describe Korium as their inaugural variable font, created in collaboration with typeface designer Valerio Monopoli. They note that it blends humanist elements with condensed, sleek and assertive forms, and that its name is inspired by corium, a lava-like material produced in nuclear reactor meltdowns. The character set reflects this theme with nuclear and scientific symbols throughout.

name: Faust
classification_notes: Faust features deliberately deformed serifs and dramatic, wide alternates, placing it firmly in the display category with an experimental serif structure drawn for expressive, editorial use rather than text settings.
summary: An experimental display typeface by Bouk Ra with deliberately deformed serifs and wide alternates, available in two named styles drawn from the Faust legend.
description: Designed by Bouk Ra, the family comes in two distinct styles, Wagner and Mephisto, each with a matching italic, giving four files in total. The deformed serifs and wide alternate characters create an uneven, theatrical rhythm suited to posters, editorial spreads and branding that needs an overtly dark or literary register. A glyph set of 450 includes ligatures, fractions, four stylistic alternate sets and broad Latin Extended diacritic coverage.
foundry_blurb: Type Department describe Faust as an experimental typeface capturing the agony and corruption of the legendary German character. Its deformed serifs and wide alternates produce a dramatic and intense rhythm, and the two styles, Wagner and Mephisto, each with matching italics, are positioned for editorial work, posters, branding and other creative applications.

name: Longinus
classification_notes: An uppercase-only display typeface with clean, bold letterforms and a futuristic aesthetic, placing it firmly in the display category with geometric tendencies drawn from anime title card design.
summary: An uppercase display typeface by Domenico Barréto, directly referencing the title sequences of Neon Genesis Evangelion.
description: Designed for logos and large-scale titles, it brings sleek, bold letterforms with a futuristic edge drawn from the dramatic aesthetic of anime intro cards. Despite its single weight and uppercase-only construction, the glyph set is broad, covering Latin Extended, Cyrillic, diacritics, ligatures, multiple numeral sets and a wide range of symbols. Best suited to branding and headline work where a bold, cinematic tone is needed.
foundry_blurb: Type Department describe Longinus as an uppercase display typeface that pays homage to Neon Genesis Evangelion, drawing inspiration from its intro sequences and title cards to capture elegance and attitude. They position it for logos and impactful titles, noting its blend of modern aesthetics with futuristic and dramatic themes.

name: Belle de Mai
classification_notes: The typeface blends classical serif elements with an edgy, expressive quality across a wide weight range, positioning it firmly as a display type rather than a text face. Its tension between delicacy and weight makes it closer to a display serif than a pure text serif.
summary: A display serif by Guille Sesenta that draws from the cultural tension between upscale and working-class Parisian neighbourhoods, spanning five weights from Light to Black.
description: Named after the La Belle de Mai neighbourhood in Paris, this typeface pairs classical serif structures with an edgy, contemporary character. The lighter weights carry a tight, delicate energy while the heavier cuts take on a softer, more rounded presence. Ten styles in total, with italics across the range, make it suited to editorial layouts, branding and display work requiring personality across varied scales.
foundry_blurb: Type Department describe Belle de Mai as a display typeface inspired by the cultural contrast between upscale inner-city areas and more affordable suburbs in cities like Paris. Designed by Guille Sesenta, it blends classical elements with a modern, edgy twist across eight weights, with lighter cuts offering delicacy and tension and heavier weights revealing a softer, more inviting character. It is positioned for editorial layouts, branding and impactful display use.

name: Kritik
classification_notes: Kritik combines angular and rounded forms in a way consistent with geometric sans-serif construction, originally conceived for an architecture magazine context, with a structured, precision-oriented aesthetic.
summary: A seven-weight sans-serif conceived for an architecture magazine, pairing angular letterforms with rounded details to reflect the dual nature of critique.
description: Designed by Daniel Stuhlpfarrer and named after the German word for criticism, Kritik juxtaposes sharp, angular characters with softer, rounded forms across seven weights from Light to Black. The concept draws on architectural metaphor, treating contrasting formal elements as structural additions to a scaffold. With old-style figures, optional ligatures and alternates across a 420-glyph set, it suits editorial design, branding and architectural publications.
foundry_blurb: Type Department describe Kritik as originally conceived for an architecture magazine, evolving into a versatile typeface at the intersection of sharp critique and constructive dialogue. The design mirrors architectural construction by adding contrasting elements to an existing structure, with angular and rounded characters coexisting across seven weights to convey both precision and expressive depth.

name: Non Natural Grotesk
classification_notes: A grotesque sans-serif with balanced counter widths and open apertures, following the conventions of classic grotesque design with a contemporary inktrap variant distinguishing it from straight revivals.
summary: A grotesque sans by Jonathan Saucedo offered in plain and inktrap variants across five weights, with open apertures and balanced counters foregrounding legibility.
description: Designed by Jonathan Saucedo, Non Natural Grotesk runs across five weights from Thin to Bold, each available in a Plain and an Inktrap style. Open apertures and balanced counter widths maintain clarity across weights and sizes, making it suitable for both screen interfaces and print. The inktrap variant introduces a subtle mechanical quality that distinguishes it from straightforward grotesque offerings.
foundry_blurb: Type Department describe Non Natural Grotesk as a typeface that appears straightforward at first glance but reveals compelling details on closer inspection, including balanced counter widths and spacious apertures designed for legibility and strong black-and-white contrast. They position it as combining contemporary details with classic grotesque style, suitable for digital interfaces and print media alike.

name: Arthemys Display Light
classification_notes: An 18th-century-influenced serif drawing on Nicolas Gando's specimen and engraved cartographic lettering, designed explicitly for display use with a revival sensibility.
summary: A display serif by Paris-based Morgane Vantorre, drawn from 18th-century specimen work and engraved cartographic lettering.
description: Rooted in Nicolas Gando's specimen and the engraved letterforms of historical cartography, this display serif carries a period character balanced by a contemporary sensibility. Its 705-glyph set covers Latin Extended with ligatures, diacritics and fractions, making it broadly usable across European languages. The single Light weight suits large-scale typesetting where delicacy and a degree of formality are both required.
foundry_blurb: Type Department describe Arthemys as a meticulous and generous serif designed by Paris-based graphic and type designer Morgane Vantorre. Born from a meeting between a Nicolas Gando specimen and engraved cartographic lettering, it unites 18th-century aesthetic with a contemporary eye, giving any text both a delicate and majestic quality.

name: Alfredino Semimono
classification_notes: A clean, geometric sans with semi-monospaced spacing and distinct style variants; the letterforms are streamlined with no serifs and draw on a geometric, slightly vintage sensibility.
summary: A single-weight geometric sans offered in three style variants, balancing a clean contemporary look with a slightly vintage character.
description: Three named styles distinguish the family: Semimono offers streamlined, unornamented letterforms with strong visual clarity; Semirounded introduces bevelled seams against defined edges for a more dynamic finish; Tuttocurvy rounds out the set with flowing, playful forms suited to illustrative contexts. The glyph set of 394 includes alternates, ligatures, diacritics and fractions across extended Latin coverage. Only a single weight is available, which limits typographic range but keeps the offering focused.
foundry_blurb: Type Department describe Alfredino Semimono as a typography project combining a dynamic, slightly vintage aesthetic with a clean contemporary look, offered in three distinct styles: Semimono for essential cleanliness and strong visual impact, Semirounded for contrasts between defined edges and bevelled seams, and Tuttocurvy for rounded, flowing shapes suited to illustrative projects.

name: Evangelion Font Family
classification_notes: A sans-serif constructed with strict geometric rigour and a flat, modular aesthetic drawn from Wim Crouwel's grid-based lettering; its display intent and bold visual character place it firmly in the display category.
summary: A geometric display sans by Domenico Barréto, drawing on Wim Crouwel's grid-based lettering and the visual identity of the Evangelion anime series.
description: Built on strict geometric construction, the typeface references Crouwel's modular, grid-driven approach to letterform design and pairs it with the angular drama associated with the Evangelion franchise. Two styles are available, Regular and Italic, with a glyph set of 525 characters covering Latin Extended, diacritics, three sets of numerals, fractions and arrows. Best suited to headlines, posters and branding where a hard-edged, constructed look is the goal.
foundry_blurb: Type Department describe Evangelion as a striking sans-serif display typeface inspired by the iconic works of Wim Crouwel and the anime series Evangelion, designed to make a bold statement and create standout headlines that captivate. They position it for projects that require a powerful, impactful and unforgettable presence.

name: Rotonto
classification_notes: Rotonto is built on grotesque proportions and construction, but introduces a distinctive tension between square-shaped counters and rounded outlines, placing it firmly in the grotesque sans-serif tradition with a geometric inflection.
summary: A variable grotesque that sets square-shaped counters against rounded outer strokes, creating a quiet but legible tension across six weights.
description: Rotonto contrasts rigid, square counters with softly rounded outlines, working within grotesque proportions to produce something slightly unusual without abandoning workhorse utility. The family runs from Light to Black with matching italics across all six weights, and is built as a variable font. Its glyph set of 373 characters covers Latin Extended with oldstyle figures, fractions and diacritics, making it workable for multilingual editorial and branding projects.
foundry_blurb: Type Department describe Rotonto as a variable typeface that accentuates the contrast between rigid, square-shaped counters and rounded outlines, rooted in the proportions and construction of a grotesque. They position it as versatile across contexts from editorial layouts to branding, with its blend of sharp edges and soft curves striking a balance suited to a wide range of design scenarios.

name: Ephidona
classification_notes: The foundry explicitly describes Ephidona as a high-contrast serif, placing it in the display serif tradition with strong thick-thin stroke differentiation suited to logotypes and editorial layouts.
summary: A high-contrast display serif with stylistic alternates and ligatures, aimed at logotype and editorial layout work.
description: Ephidona is a single-weight, high-contrast serif with a 349-glyph set covering Latin Extended, stylistic alternates, ligatures and a full punctuation suite. The strong thick-thin contrast makes it well suited to large-scale headline and logotype settings. Practical for branding and layout work where a refined, formal serif character is needed without a full weight range.
foundry_blurb: Ephidona is described by the foundry as a high-contrast serif offering a versatile toolkit for creating unique logotypes and striking layouts, with support for Latin languages, numbers, stylistic alternates, punctuation and ligatures across a 349-glyph set.

name: Good Girl
classification_notes: A heavy, condensed display typeface with dense counters and tight spacing, designed explicitly for headline and poster use with a bold typographic personality rooted in protest art aesthetics.
summary: A heavy display typeface by Marion Bisserier drawing on protest print aesthetics, available in three widths from Regular to Extra Condensed.
description: Designed by Marion Bisserier, the typeface takes visual cues from Guerrilla Girls prints and protest art, using dense counters and tight spacing across all three widths to achieve maximum typographic coverage. The compact nature of each cut makes it well suited to headlines, posters and any context requiring a loud, assertive presence. Latin Standard A diacritics are included across a 220-glyph set covering uppercase, lowercase, numerals and punctuation.
foundry_blurb: Type Department describe Good Girl as a bold and playful display typeface that challenges social norms, citing Guerrilla Girls prints and protest art as visual references. Its small twenty-unit counters and narrow spacing create a compact, dense character consistent across the family, making it suited to bold statements in headlines, posters and impactful design work across print and digital platforms.

name: Metrik
classification_notes: Described as a refined grotesque sans serif rooted in Swiss typographic tradition with subtle humanist qualities, placing it firmly in the grotesque category with humanist inflections.
summary: A five-weight grotesque from Type Department that draws on Swiss typographic tradition whilst introducing subtle humanist qualities for added warmth.
description: Rooted in the Swiss grotesque lineage, Metrik offers clean lines and balanced proportions across five weights from Light to Black, each with a matching italic. Subtle humanist details distinguish it from more neutral Swiss models, giving it a quiet personality suited to editorial layouts, branding and screen use. At 352 glyphs per style, the character set covers extended Latin diacritics and includes oldstyle figures alongside standard numerals.
foundry_blurb: Type Department describe Metrik as a refined grotesque sans serif available in five weights, blending flexibility with unexpected character. They position it as rooted in Swiss typographic tradition yet informed by modern influences, with subtle humanist qualities that bridge traditional Swiss sans serifs and contemporary aesthetics. The foundry highlight its suitability across editorial, branding and web contexts, and note its ability to perform in both text and display settings.

name: Lay Grotesk
classification_notes: Explicitly positioned as a contemporary reimagining of milestone grotesques including Helvetica and Neue Haas Grotesk, drawing on Swiss typeface principles with softened contrast and geometric terminations.
summary: A Swiss-influenced grotesque from Due Studio that softens the rigidity of Helvetica and its peers with reduced letter width and straight terminations at display sizes.
description: Designed by Due Studio, Lay Grotesk takes the neutral tradition of Helvetica, Neue Haas Grotesk and Folio as its starting point, then adjusts contrast and letter width to improve readability at small sizes while letting the geometric character emerge at larger settings. The family runs five weights from Regular to Black with no italics, and 796 glyphs cover Latin Extended-A alongside a full suite of OpenType features including nine stylistic sets and discretionary ligatures. The formal tension between soft and rigid proportions makes it usable across both text and display contexts without committing strongly to either.
foundry_blurb: Type Department describe Lay Grotesk as a contemporary sans serif that reimagines classic grotesques such as Helvetica and Neue Haas Grotesk. Drawing from Swiss typeface principles, it maintains neutrality while softening their rigidity through reduced contrast. Its slightly reduced letter width aids readability at small sizes, while at display sizes the geometric essence becomes apparent through straight terminations. The name 'Lay' reflects a universal appeal, and the typeface ships with 796 glyphs, extensive OpenType support, and numerous discretionary ligatures.

name: Arose (Free)
classification_notes: A bold display typeface with strong thick-thin contrast and a structured grid system, placing it firmly in the display category with high-contrast characteristics evoking 1970s lettering and signage.
summary: A high-contrast display typeface built on a strict 3:1:3 grid, drawing on 1970s lettering and signage with a structured, contemporary edge.
description: Designed by Celia Bayo, Arose pairs dense weight with fine hairlines across a rigid 3:1:3 grid, producing the thick-thin drama of vintage 1970s signage within a more controlled, contemporary framework. The single bold weight is aimed at headlines, posters and editorial typographic elements where expressive contrast is needed. Available free for both personal and commercial use via the Type Department newsletter.
foundry_blurb: Type Department describe Arose as a monumental display typeface created by Celia Bayo, characterised by expressive boldness and strong contrasts between dense weight and thin lines that evoke a nostalgic tension reminiscent of 1970s lettering and signage. The typeface follows a strict 3:1:3 grid to balance its grooviness with a more contemporary, refined tone, making it suited to headlines, posters and typographic editorial elements.

name: Booty Bold
classification_notes: A slightly condensed sans-serif with consistent stroke weight across uppercase and lowercase, evoking a monospaced digital aesthetic rooted in geometric construction rather than humanist or grotesque traditions.
summary: A slightly condensed bold sans by Berlin-based Nat Brown, drawn from the visual culture of regional American club music and its celebration of voluptuous figures.
description: Designed by Nat Brown, the typeface takes cues from the energy and aesthetic of American regional club music, channelling a tension between nostalgia and futurism. Consistent stroke weight across upper and lowercase gives it a quasi-monospaced, digital quality that reads as distinctly contemporary. It is best deployed at large sizes in headlines, posters and logotypes where its cultural specificity and bold presence can do the most work.
foundry_blurb: Type Department describe Booty Bold as a slightly condensed sans-serif inspired by the vibrant culture of regional American club music and its celebration of voluptuous figures. They note its consistent weight across upper and lowercase lends a digital, near-monospaced aesthetic, and recommend it for headlines, posters and logotypes where its bold, dynamic character can shine.

name: Arkive
classification_notes: The typeface features geometric and linear stylistic alternates as a core design characteristic, placing it firmly in the geometric sans-serif tradition, with a clean construction suited to both text and display use.
summary: A geometric sans with an extensive set of linear and geometric stylistic alternates, spanning seven weights with italics for use across body text and logotype work.
description: Originally released as Catalogue, Arkive covers seven weights from Thin to Bold, each with a matching italic, across a full Latin Extended character set. Its defining feature is a rich collection of geometric and linear alternates that allow the tone to shift from restrained to expressive without changing typeface. Ligatures, lining tabular figures and cryptocurrency symbol support round out a practical feature set.
foundry_blurb: Type Department describe Arkive as crafted with versatility and creativity in mind, designed to perform across applications from body text to logotypes. Its extensive geometric and linear stylistic alternates allow users to tailor the appearance to suit their needs, whether for subtle refinement or bold statement.

name: Gosna
classification_notes: Insufficient page data to determine subcategory; classified broadly as display based on the typeface name and foundry context alone.
summary: A typeface from Type Department for which detailed specification data could not be retrieved at the time of cataloguing.
description: The page for Gosna at Type Department failed to load, leaving classification, weight range and language support unverifiable. The entry has been created with placeholder values and should be revisited once the product page is accessible. Type Department is a contemporary foundry whose catalogue tends toward display and expressive Latin typefaces.
foundry_blurb: No foundry copy was retrievable due to a page load failure.

name: Pexel Grotesk
classification_notes: Pexel Grotesk is a pixel-grid display typeface that deliberately recreates grotesque proportions within bitmap constraints, placing it firmly in the display category with a pixel/bitmap subcategory rather than a conventional grotesque.
summary: A pixel-grid typeface that maps grotesque proportions onto a strict pixel grid, with rounded edges that set it apart from typical bitmap fonts.
description: Built on a strict pixel grid, Pexel Grotesk takes the proportions and structure of traditional grotesque typefaces and renders them in pixel art form. Rounded edges distinguish it from the hard corners typical of the genre, lending it a softer character suited to both retro-themed work and contemporary digital contexts. The glyph set runs to 236 characters, covering uppercase, lowercase, diacritics, punctuation, arrows and seven custom symbols.
foundry_blurb: Type Department describe Pexel Grotesk as driven by a passion for precision and character in pixel art, recreating the proportions and essence of traditional grotesque typefaces within a strict pixel grid. They highlight rounded edges as a distinctive feature rarely found in pixel fonts, and position the typeface's 236-character glyph set and OpenType features as making it a refreshingly well-crafted option balancing retro charm with modern functionality.

name: Galipos
classification_notes: Galipos is classified as a display typeface with sharp, intricate letterforms and contrasting edges and curves; it sits outside conventional serif or sans-serif categories, functioning as an experimental display face.
summary: A sharp, culturally rooted display typeface by Guille Sesenta, drawing on Andalusian heritage with contrasting edges and delicate curves suited to editorial and poster work.
description: Designed by Guille Sesenta, Galipos pairs angular edges with delicate curves to create a visual tension that the designer frames around Andalusian culture and its particular mix of resilience and refinement. The family is currently offered in Light and Light Italic, with 596 glyphs covering Latin Extended and Cyrillic, stylistic alternates and ligatures. Its character suits headline-led editorial layouts and identity work where a strong typographic personality is needed.
foundry_blurb: Type Department describe Galipos as a distinctive display typeface inspired by Andalusian society and culture, blending historical depth with contemporary relevance through sharp, intricate details that convey both strength and delicacy. Each glyph is designed to reflect the resilience and complexity of its cultural inspiration, with sharp edges and delicate curves capturing what the foundry calls the Andalusian spirit. They position it as ideal for impactful headlines, posters and editorial layouts.
